items.final_wording: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

items.rationale: The exact NICE early-pregnancy section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, concerns, clinician risk context, measured vital data, focused assessment, appearance and time-critical symptoms without diagnosing ectopic pregnancy or miscarriage.

items.final_wording: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.7.6 — ultrasound for pregnancy location and viability

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

items.rationale: The exact ultrasound section supports recording existing tests and imaging already performed or reviewed, without recommending or interpreting them.

items.final_wording: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.10 — hCG in pregnancy of unknown location

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

items.rationale: The exact hCG and safety section supports documenting clinician-decided escalation, specialist discussion and arranged handover or follow-up, without setting thresholds or intervals.

items.final_wording: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

items.rationale: The exact service section supports a clinician-entered plan and completed risk or consent discussion only when documented; it does not create management.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

added_items.rationale: The exact NICE early-pregnancy section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, concerns, clinician risk context, measured vital data, focused assessment, appearance and time-critical symptoms without diagnosing ectopic pregnancy or miscarriage.

added_items.final_wording: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.7.6 — ultrasound for pregnancy location and viability

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

added_items.rationale: The exact ultrasound section supports recording existing tests and imaging already performed or reviewed, without recommending or interpreting them.

added_items.final_wording: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.10 — hCG in pregnancy of unknown location

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

added_items.rationale: The exact hCG and safety section supports documenting clinician-decided escalation, specialist discussion and arranged handover or follow-up, without setting thresholds or intervals.

added_items.final_wording: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.

exact_location.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

added_items.rationale: The exact service section supports a clinician-entered plan and completed risk or consent discussion only when documented; it does not create management.
